title: lazy-sunday xintense jamaican peas bain-marie
description: A shaggy, savory, and comforting Jamaican dish featuring split peas, onions, and a bain-marie. Perfect for a relaxing Sunday morning.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened and translucent, about 8 minutes.
Add the garam masala, thyme, allspice, cinnamon, scotch bonnet peppers, salt, and black pepper. Cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the split peas, water, coconut milk, nutmeg, and ginger powder. Stir well to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and cover with a lid.
Create a bain-marie by placing the saucepan in a larger saucepan or Dutch oven filled with water to a depth of 2-3 cm.
Bring the water in the larger saucepan to a simmer and cook for 30-40 minutes, or until the peas are tender.
Season with additional salt and pepper to taste, if needed.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs or scallions, if desired.
